description:
The ransomware group known as Cl0p is a variant of a previously known strain dubbed CryptoMix. It is worth noting that this variant was delivered as the final payload in a phishing campaign in 2019 and was exclusively financially motivated, with attacks carried out by the threat actors TA505.

At that time, malicious actors sent phishing emails that led to a macro-enabled document that would drop a loader called 'Get2.' After gaining an initial foothold in the system or infrastructure, the actors began using reconnaissance, lateral movement, and exfiltration techniques to prepare for the deployment of the ransomware.

After the execution of the ransomware, Cl0p appends the extension '.clop' to the end of files, or other types of extensions such as '.CIIp, .Cllp, and .C_L_O_P,' as well as different versions of the ransom note that were also observed after encryption. Depending on the variant, any of the ransom text files were created with names like 'ClopReadMe.txt, README_README.txt, Cl0pReadMe.txt, and READ_ME_!!!.TXT.'

The Clop operation has shifted from delivering its final payload via phishing and has begun initiating attacks using vulnerabilities that resulted in the exploitation and infection of victims' infrastructures.
Source: https://github.com/crocodyli/ThreatActors-TTPs

techniques.technique_details: Arrives via phishing emails that have Get2 Loader, which will download the SDBot and FlawedAmmy RAT.
Arrives via any the following exploits: CVE-2021-27101, CVE-2021-27102, CVE-2021-27103, CVE-2021-27104, CVE-2021-35211.
Have been reported to make use of compromised accounts to access victims via RDP.
Uses native API to execute various commands/routines.
Uses various scripting interpreters like PowerShell, Windows command shell and Visual Basic (macro in documents).
User execution is needed to carry out the payload from the spear-phishing link/attachments.
Creates registry run entries to execute the ransomware as a service.

techniques.technique_name: Create or modify system process: Windows service
techniques.technique_details: Creates a service to execute the ransomware.

techniques.technique_name: Domain Policy modification: Group Policy modification
techniques.technique_details: Uses stolen credentials to access the AD servers to gain administrator privilege and attack other machines within the network.
Makes use of CVE-2021-27102 to escalate privilege.
UAC bypass.
Makes use of the following digital signatures: DVERI, FADO, TOV.

techniques.technique_name: Impair defenses: disable or modify tools
techniques.technique_details: Disables security-related software by terminating them.
The tool used for exfiltration has a part of its malware trace removal, and it drops a base-64 encoded file.

techniques.technique_name: Indicator removal on host: file deletion
techniques.technique_details: Deletes traces of itself in the infected machine.
To deliver other tools and payload, a tool has the capability to inject its downloaded payload.
A startup script runs just before the system gets to the login screen via startup registry.

techniques.technique_name: Indicator removal on host: clear Windows event logs
techniques.technique_details: Clears the Event Viewer log files.
Searches for specific files and the directory related to its encryption.
Makes use of tools for network scans.
Discovers certain processes for process termination.
Identifies keyboard layout and other system information.
Queries certain registries as part of its routine.
Discovers security software for reconnaissance and termination.
Can make use of RDP to transfer the ransomware or tools within the network.
Drops a copy of the payload to the compromised AD and then creates a service on the target machine to execute the copy of the payload.
Might make use of RDP to manually search for valuable files or information.
Uses http/s to communicate to its C&C server.
DEWMODE web shell extracts list of available files from a MySQL database on the FTA and lists these files and corresponding their metadata. These will then be downloaded using the DEWMODE web shell.
Uses a combination of Salsa20, AES, and ECDH to encrypt the files and key.
Deletes shadow copies.
